message:
ft-xbnl0.2.3: route legacy run_with_auth/native listener through explicit-Cx paths

Wire the legacy public entry points so that they root a single
request-level Cx for the server/listener's full lifetime instead of
manufacturing a fresh Cx per shutdown poll (which breaks the
cancellation chain under asupersync-runtime).

Specifically:

* crates/frankenterm-core/src/ipc.rs
  - `run_with_auth`, `run_with_registry_and_auth`, and
    `run_with_registry_auth_rpc_and_search_config` now branch on the
    `asupersync-runtime` cfg: under asupersync they obtain one
    `crate::cx::for_request()` Cx and hand off to the explicit-Cx
    `*_with_cx` variants; under tokio they keep the legacy
    IpcHandlerContext path unchanged.
  - Adds a test
    `ipc_server_run_with_auth_legacy_entry_exits_cleanly_on_shutdown`
    that binds via the legacy entry point, drives a shutdown via the
    channel, and asserts the socket is cleaned up — guarding against
    regression if the legacy path stops threading through the cx-first
    accept loop.

* crates/frankenterm-core/src/native_events.rs
  - `NativeEventListener::run` routes to `run_with_cx` under
    asupersync so the listener keeps a single request-rooted
    cancellation chain; the tokio JoinSet-accept loop is preserved
    behind `cfg(not(feature = "asupersync-runtime"))`.
  - `dispatch_event_with_timeout` similarly delegates to
    `dispatch_event_with_timeout_with_cx`; the tokio reserve/commit
    branch stays untouched (still using a fresh reserve Cx, as before).
  - Adds a test
    `dispatch_event_with_timeout_with_precancelled_cx_reports_backpressure`
    that pre-cancels the Cx and verifies the dispatch returns
    `Backpressure` rather than surfacing the cancellation as a different
    outcome.

No behavioral change on the tokio side. Under asupersync, cancellations
now propagate cleanly from a single request Cx through the IPC accept
loop and the native-event dispatch path, which is the ft-xbnl0.2.3
contract.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
